seoTitle: Glenroy vs Glenugie — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Glenroy and Glenugie. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Glenroy and Glenugie.
heading: Glenroy vs Glenugie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Glenroy, New South Wales and Glenugie, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Glenugie has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Glenroy (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Glenroy or Glenugie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Glenroy has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Glenugie scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Glenroy scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Glenugie scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Glenroy or Glenugie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Glenugie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
